text.title: monolith-architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for monolith-architecture as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Business logic in the persistence layer (fat repositories or stored procedures)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Makes business rules untestable without a running database and couples domain changes to schema changes

failure-mode.description: Deploying database migrations and application code in a single step
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: If the migration fails mid-deploy, the application is in an inconsistent state. Always deploy migrate-then-upgrade: migration first (backward-compatible), then new application version.
